claims.claim: CSS masonry layout can be implemented using existing grid syntax by adding only one new value to the grid-template-rows or grid-template-columns property, without requiring a new display type.
claims.quote: The Just Use Grid option adds _only_ _one new value_ to CSS

claims.claim: There are two competing specification proposals for CSS masonry as of late 2024: a 'Grid Integrated' option (display: grid; grid-template-rows: masonry) and a 'Grid Independent' option (display: masonry; masonry-template-tracks: ...).
claims.quote: Currently, the specification is drafted with two options, going head-to-head — the 'Grid Integrated' option and the 'Grid Independent' option.

claims.claim: The 'New Masonry Layout' (Grid Independent) option duplicates approximately 10 existing CSS Grid properties under masonry-prefixed names (e.g., grid-column becomes masonry-track, grid-template-columns becomes masonry-template-tracks).
claims.quote: The 'New Masonry Layout' option creates ~10 duplicate properties mapping Grid concepts to masonry equivalents

claims.claim: Performance concerns about integrating masonry with full CSS Grid power were resolved in the six months prior to October 2024, making the Grid Integrated approach technically viable.
claims.quote: Over the last six months, the key performance concerns have been addressed. Yes, it is possible to integrate Masonry layout with the full power of CSS Grid.

claims.claim: As of October 2024, CSS masonry was available in Safari Technology Preview and Firefox (flag-enabled since v77), but the Chrome team had published a position against the unified Grid syntax.
claims.quote: Safari: Available in Safari Technology Preview and Safari 17.x/18.x with feature flag enabled. Firefox: Supported in Firefox 77+ with flag enabled. Chrome: Team has published position against unified Grid syntax.
